channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Persona 4 Revival Release Date Revealed During XBOX Games Showcase 2026
item.content:encoded:
Persona 4 Revival has been given an official release date of February 18, 2027, during the XBOX Games Showcase 2026.

The highly anticipated remake of Persona 4 was announced at last year’s event, though details surrounding the return of Teddie and co. have been kept under close wraps—until now.

More Persona 4 Revival Details

As you can imagine, Persona 4 Revival might be a from-the-ground-up remake, but it hasn’t lost any of the original’s sauce. The new trailer showcased plenty of the og settings, and each one looks as visually striking as before, with a significant bump bringing the game in line with Persona 5.

If you’re a relatively new fan to the Persona franchise, and you’ve only played Persona 5, then Persona 4 Revival is looking to be the perfect entry point for one of the most beloved games in the series to date—and it should tide you over until Persona 6 eventually rolls around.

Check out the brand new trailer for yourself below:

This was just one of the many announcements during the XBOX Games Showcase. If you’re looking to stay up to date on everything else, check out our live updates for the showcase here.

What do you make of Persona 4 Revival? If you played the original or remaster, will you be jumping back in? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra not arriving in 2026, as well as everything that was announced during Summer Game Fest.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
During the latest iteration of the XBOX Games Showcase, the developers finally announced the early access and release dates for Halo Campaign Evolved, with the title set to arrive in July 2026.

The last time players received new details about Halo Campaign Evolved was in October 2025, when the developers released a 13-minute gameplay demo and announced the PS5 release. Now, the day has finally come to let players know when they can dive into this remake.

Halo Campaign Evolved Release Date Set for July 2026

During the latest showcase event, Halo Campaign Evolved received another full-blown trailer, and at the end, players were informed of the release and early access dates. The game is set to release on July 28, 2026. The announcement also informed players that It will be available Day One with Game Pass.

The upcoming title is a modernized remake of Halo: Combat Evolved’s campaign, allowing players to experience the original story with high-definition visuals, updated cinematics, and refined controls, along with three new prequel missions featuring the Master Chief and Sgt. Johnson. The campaign is available to play solo or in a 4-player online co-op mode, featuring full crossplay and cross-progression support. This makes it an excellent option for long-time fans returning to the series, while also serving as an inviting entry point for new players, particularly those on PlayStation.

In other news, a new report details the next mainline campaign project from Halo Studios and includes a job listing. Additionally, Halo Studios is apparently hard at work trying to deliver the original trilogy all over again, with Halo 1, Halo 2, and Halo 3 Remakes. What are your thoughts on Halo Campaign Evolved’s release date? Will you be playing it on XBOX or PS5? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Stuntman Hollywood not being allowed to show Jurassic Park in the reveal trailer. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The Coalition and XBOX Game Studios revealed the October release date for Gears of War E-Day at the XBOX Games Showcase 2026.

Regarding the release of Gears of War: E-Day, there has been considerable confusion about whether the game would be available on PlayStation 5. According to the latest announcement, it appears that a PS5 release is not on the cards as it is an XBOX console exclusive. However, the game will launch soon on Xbox Series X/S and PC.

Gears of War: E-Day Releases on (insert date)

The Coalition provided players with a brand-new gameplay trailer for Gears of War: E-Day at the XBOX Games Showcase. Towards the end, they announced the release date for the upcoming title on October 6, 2026. Gears E-Day pre-orders go live today, right after the showcase. The title will also be available Day One on Game Pass

The trailer showcased extensive gameplay, featuring Marcus fighting numerous monsters, armed only with makeshift weapons he found along the way and without any protective gear. The city lay in ruins, with derailed trains scattered around and a horde of monsters emerging from the ground, some wielding their own weapons. Additionally, we saw a massive claw-like figure erupting from the earth.

About Gears of War E-Day

For those unaware, the reason there’s so much hype for the upcoming installment, Gears of War: E-Day, is that it arrives as a prequel, set 14 years before Gears of War. Popular characters Marcus Fenix and Dom Santiago make an appearance again, this time returning home to face a new threat: the Locust Horde. The Locust Horde is a subterranean monster that is laying siege to humanity itself.

In other news, here’s everything that was announced at the latest iteration of XBOX Games Showcase. Additionally, the Gears of War Netflix movie adaptation has received a promising update from the director. Will you be playing Gears of War E-Day, when it releases on October 6? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Avatar: The Last Airbender RPG no longer moving forward. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: XBOX Games Showcase 2026—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
Xbox and Microsoft could be set for one of their most pivotal presentations in years. The XBOX Games Showcase 2026 could be a huge sign of things to come for the green brand, so don’t miss a single reveal or trailer.

In the blink of an eye, Summer Game Fest 2026 is coming to an end. Already, we’ve been treated to a plethora of stellar spectacles: The Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase, the Sony State of Play June 2026 Showcase, and The MIX.

It’s XBOX‘s turn.

Everything Announced During XBOX Games Showcase 2026

Bad Magpie

Fable

Gears of War: E-Day

Halo: Campaign Evolved

Join Us

Metro 2039

Persona 4 Revival

Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy

Sea of Thieves Update

State of Decay 3

Wo Long 2: Wings of Ember

Let us know which game you’re most excited for and what you hope to see from the XBOX Games Showcase 2026. You can do this and talk about it live with our community via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

Gears E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved Preorders To Go Live Today

Gears of War: E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved’s preorders will go live right after today’s showcase. Here are more details.

Gears of War: E-Day PS5 Version Debate Sparks Online

There’s some confusion about Gears of War: E-Day’s PS5 release, with some claiming it won’t happen and others suggesting it might.

Avatar: The Last Airbender RPG No Longer Moving Forward

Paramount Games Studio executive comments on game projects no longer in development related to its IPs.

Little Nightmares 3 ‘The Backstage’ DLC Coming Next Week

The Backstage will soon be available in Little Nightmares 3. The new DLC expansion drops next week.
